_about: M5 acceptance fixture (job-0043): Hurricane Ian flood-on-Fort Myers demo parameters. The bbox is a small (~13 km × 16 km, ~210 km²) coastal slice covering downtown Fort Myers + Caloosahatchee mouth — the area inundated by Ian's storm surge on 2022-09-28. ATCF Hurricane Ian forcing integration is deferred per OQ-42-ATCF-HURRICANE-IAN-INTEGRATION; v0.1 demo substitutes the Atlas 14 100-yr / 24-hr design storm (live-verified at 11.9 inches by job-0042 against the real NOAA PFDS endpoint). The substrate verification — that the chain runs end-to-end through the deployed Cloud Workflows orchestrator — is the M5 acceptance criterion regardless of which forcing is wired.

_substrate_notes.nlcd_substrate: Tier 2 MRLC WCS 1.0.0 GetCoverage (post-job-0044 hotfix; canonical NLCD class integers, not palette indices). NLCD vintage 2021 expected; fetched classes for Fort Myers expected to be a clean subset of manning_mapping.csv v1.0.0's 20 mapped classes (gate PASSES).
_substrate_notes.hydromt_status: HydroMT-SFINCS is NOT installed in the dev .venv-agent (heavyweight dep that the production job-0040 SFINCS container has). The expected outcome on the dev box is `HYDROMT_UNAVAILABLE` — the chain runs through fetchers + NLCD gate PASS + forcing + lands on build_sfincs_model's `import hydromt_sfincs` guard. Substrate-verification outcome the kickoff explicitly accepts.
_substrate_notes.cloud_workflows_orchestrator: projects/grace-2-hazard-prod/locations/us-central1/workflows/grace-2-sfincs-orchestrator (job-0040). The agent's `run_solver` would dispatch to this if `build_sfincs_model` succeeded.
_substrate_notes.cache_buckets: gs://grace-2-hazard-prod-cache (fetcher writes — static-30d TTL class); gs://grace-2-hazard-prod-runs (SFINCS run outputs — managed by Cloud Run Job + orchestrator).
